summary: Partially covered: the 5 "headline" contracts (Workspace, ProviderMatrix, AgentJob, KnowledgeMutationPlan, ExternalWriteEnvelope) have TS field sketches, but ~9 of the 15 required cross-track contracts are referenced-only with no field definition and no owning §. Two of the undefined ones (EgressPolicy, ToolPolicy) gate P0 security/DoD controls, so this dimension has critical gaps before contract-track freeze.

findings.finding: EgressPolicy is a required cross-track contract that gates a P0 security/DoD control (Employer Work raw-content egress acknowledgment), yet it is never defined with fields. It appears only as a type reference (Workspace.egressPolicy: EgressPolicy) in DATA_MODEL.md and PRD §10.2; its shape (permitted processors, per-processor raw-content-allowed flag, acknowledgment/opt-in state, enforcement point) is nowhere specified.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md L48 (egressPolicy: EgressPolicy) and ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md Appendix A (no EgressPolicy row); PRD L617 'egressPolicy: EgressPolicy // permitted external processors (§16.5)' and L995/L1213 (A4) describe semantics but no field-level contract
findings.proposed_fix: In final §3/§5 add a concrete EgressPolicy contract: { workspaceId, allowedProcessors: ProcessorId[], rawContentAllowedProcessors: ProcessorId[], employerRawEgressAcknowledged: boolean, acknowledgedAt? } with an owning §5 (Policy/Security/Egress), and define the relationship to ProviderMatrix.rawCloudEgressEnabled (currently a duplicate/overlapping flag).
findings.anchor: §5 Policy, Security, and Egress (+ §3 contract listing)

findings.finding: ToolPolicy is referenced as a shared contract and as AgentJob.toolPolicy but is never defined with fields. The ING-7 job-admission gate (P0) must REJECT any untrusted-content job 'declaring a mutating tool' — that gate cannot be specified or tested without a defined ToolPolicy shape (allowed tool set, read-only marker, mutating classification).
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §2.5 shared-contracts list (L116) and DATA_MODEL.md L67 (toolPolicy: ToolPolicy) reference it; PRD ING-7 L524 and AgentJob §13.1 L800 require it; no ToolPolicy field definition exists in any artifact, and Appendix A has no ToolPolicy row
findings.proposed_fix: Define ToolPolicy in §3 (e.g. { mode: 'read_only' | 'scoped_write', allowedTools: ToolId[], deniedTools: ToolId[], allowsMutating: boolean }) and state in §5/§7 the admission-gate predicate that rejects untrusted-content jobs whose ToolPolicy admits any mutating tool.

findings.finding: ProviderRoute is a core routing contract (AgentJob.providerRoute, ProviderMatrix.capabilityDefaults: Record<Capability, ProviderRoute>) but its fields are never defined anywhere. Without it the provider-matrix and per-job routing contracts are unbuildable.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md L54 (capabilityDefaults: Record<Capability, ProviderRoute>) and L68 (providerRoute: ProviderRoute); no ProviderRoute definition in DATA_MODEL, DOMAIN_MODEL, draft, or PRD; no Appendix A row
findings.proposed_fix: Add ProviderRoute to §3/§7 with fields such as { providerId: ProviderId, model: string, runtime: 'claude-agent-sdk' | 'hermes' | 'deterministic', endpointRef?, egressClass: 'cloud' | 'local', costDefaults? }.

findings.finding: Capability is the key type for the entire provider matrix and AgentJob routing, but the architecture treats it as an opaque type with no enumeration. The PRD enumerates exactly six values; the draft/DATA_MODEL never pin them, so Record<Capability, ProviderRoute> and capability-keyed conformance tests have no defined key set.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md L54/L62 use Capability opaquely; PRD §13.1 L796 enumerates 'meeting.close | daily.brief | project.sync | source.ingest | task.route | notebooklm.sync'; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md never lists the capability set
findings.proposed_fix: Enumerate the Capability union in §3 (reconciled with PRD §13.1's six values), and note whether the set is closed for V1 (provider conformance and the matrix are keyed off it).

findings.finding: ProposedAction has only a prose field-summary (no TS sketch) and its relationship to ExternalWriteEnvelope is undefined. Both carry targetSystem/canonicalObjectKey/idempotencyKey, but the mapping from agent-emitted ProposedAction to the Tool-Gateway ExternalWriteEnvelope (who fills idempotency/precondition/payloadHash, and when) is never specified — a load-bearing seam between the providers and integrations tracks.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md L19 (ProposedAction summary), KnowledgeMutationPlan.externalActionProposals: ProposedAction[] in DATA_MODEL.md L82; ExternalWriteEnvelope defined separately in DATA_MODEL.md L86-95; PRD §13.1 L812 AgentResult.proposedActions; no ProposedAction TS and no ProposedAction->ExternalWriteEnvelope transform documented
findings.proposed_fix: Add a ProposedAction TS contract and a §8 subsection defining the deterministic ProposedAction -> ExternalWriteEnvelope construction (which side computes canonicalObjectKey/idempotencyKey/payloadHash and when approvalId is bound).

findings.finding: AgentResult — the runtime output contract carrying status, output, citations, proposedActions, and knowledgeMutationPlan — is defined in the PRD but is entirely absent from DATA_MODEL.md, DOMAIN_MODEL.md, the §2.5 shared-contract list, and Appendix A. It is the cross-track hand-off from the provider broker to KnowledgeWriter/Tool Gateway and must be a frozen contract.
findings.evidence: PRD §13.1 L806-815 defines AgentResult; not present in DATA_MODEL.md Core Contract Sketches, DOMAIN_MODEL.md entity table, ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §2.5 (L116) or Appendix A (L441-451)
findings.proposed_fix: Add AgentResult to §3/§7 and Appendix A, including its status enum (succeeded | failed | needs_clarification | needs_approval) and the schema-gate boundary that converts it to candidate KnowledgeMutationPlan/ProposedAction.
findings.anchor: §7 Provider and Runtime Broker (+ Appendix A)

findings.finding: SourceEnvelope is a required shared contract with no TS field definition, and there is an unreconciled three-name collision: DOMAIN_MODEL entity 'Source', shared contract 'SourceEnvelope', and reference type 'SourceRef' (used in KnowledgeMutationPlan.sourceRefs and AgentResult.citations). Their relationship and which is persisted vs referenced is never stated.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §2.5 L116 + Appendix A L448 list SourceEnvelope by summary only; DOMAIN_MODEL.md L13 defines 'Source' entity; DATA_MODEL.md L80/L100 use SourceRef and 'SourceEnvelope is registered before extraction'; USER_FLOWS.md L67 and PRD L715 enumerate SourceEnvelope fields; no TS contract and no Source/SourceEnvelope/SourceRef reconciliation
findings.proposed_fix: Define SourceEnvelope (sourceId, workspaceId, origin, type, contentHash, sensitivity, userTags, routingHints) and SourceRef as a TS contract in §3, and state explicitly that Source(entity) is the persisted record, SourceEnvelope the registration contract, SourceRef the pointer.

findings.finding: WorkflowRunRef is in the §2.5 shared-contract list but is defined nowhere and is missing from Appendix A; meanwhile DOMAIN_MODEL names a 'WorkflowRun' entity and AgentJob.workflowRunId is typed as a bare string. The cross-track audit/workflow reference contract is thus three inconsistent things (WorkflowRunRef vs WorkflowRun vs workflowRunId: string).
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §2.5 L116 lists WorkflowRunRef; DOMAIN_MODEL.md L22 'WorkflowRun' entity; DATA_MODEL.md L60 'workflowRunId: string'; no WorkflowRunRef definition and no Appendix A row
findings.proposed_fix: Define WorkflowRunRef ({ workflowId, runId, trigger? }) in §3, use it (not a bare string) in AgentJob/AuditRecord, and reconcile naming with the WorkflowRun entity.

findings.finding: AuditRecord — a production-grade required contract — has only a prose field summary and no TS/schema definition, despite the build posture making audit a non-deferred concern. Fields (actor, event, refs, payloadHash, before/after summary, timestamps) are listed but not pinned to a contract or an immutability/tombstone schema.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md L24 + ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md Appendix A L451 give summary only; DATA_MODEL.md L28 Audit domain says 'immutable/tombstoned for deletion'; no TS contract; §15 lists audit as 'Not deferred' (L377)
findings.proposed_fix: Add a concrete AuditRecord TS contract in §3/§4 with the immutability + deletion-tombstone fields, and reference the WorkflowRunRef/SourceRef/Approval ref types it carries.

findings.finding: The DATA_MODEL Workspace sketch drops several load-bearing routing fields present in the PRD §10.2 Workspace: calendars (CalendarAccount[]), taskSystems (TaskSystemMapping), notebookPolicy (NotebookPolicy), and gbrainSourceId. These feed connector routing and the GBrain brain-vs-source decision, so the architecture's Workspace contract is silently narrower than ground truth.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md L39-49 Workspace omits calendars/taskSystems/notebookPolicy/gbrainSourceId; PRD §10.2 L606-618 includes them
findings.proposed_fix: Reconcile the §3 Workspace contract with PRD §10.2 — either re-add calendars/taskSystems/notebookPolicy/gbrainSourceId or explicitly state they are normalized into separate config contracts and link them.

findings.finding: Numerous referenced sub-contracts are used inside the defined sketches but never themselves defined, leaving the contract track unable to freeze: NoteCreate, NotePatch, LinkMutation, FrontmatterPatch (KnowledgeMutationPlan), WriteReceipt (ExternalWriteEnvelope/PRD §16.1), ContextRef (AgentJob), ProviderId, and PRD-side ExternalRef/CalendarAccount/TaskSystemMapping/NotebookPolicy/ProgressProvider/AgentLogEntry.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md L73-95 (NoteCreate/NotePatch/LinkMutation/FrontmatterPatch/WriteReceipt), L65/L63 (ContextRef/ProviderId); PRD §10.2 L614-633 and §13.1 L798-814; none defined in any artifact
findings.proposed_fix: Add a §3 'sub-contracts' subsection (or a contracts appendix) defining each referenced type, or explicitly mark which are deferred to the contracts-track implementation with a placeholder schema id.

findings.finding: Appendix A 'Model / Contract Inventory' is incomplete relative to the §2.5 shared-contract list and the contracts this dimension must cover: it has no rows for ToolPolicy, ProposedAction, Capability, ProviderRoute, EgressPolicy, WorkflowRunRef, or AgentResult, and several existing rows give no owning § for the undefined ones. The owning-§ assignment that finalize requires is therefore missing for ~half the required contracts.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md Appendix A L441-451 (rows: Workspace, ProviderMatrix, AgentJob, KnowledgeMutationPlan, ExternalWriteEnvelope, SourceEnvelope, GclProjection, Approval, AuditRecord) vs §2.5 list L116 and task contract set
findings.proposed_fix: Expand Appendix A into the canonical contract registry: one row per required contract (incl. ToolPolicy, ProposedAction, Capability, ProviderRoute, EgressPolicy, WorkflowRunRef, AgentResult), each with full fields and a single owning §; make it the index the contracts track freezes against.

findings.finding: GclProjection and Approval are listed with prose field summaries but no TS sketch, and the visibility enum is not reconciled: Workspace.defaultVisibility uses isolated|coordination|sanitized|full, while GclProjection 'visibility' and the per-note frontmatter cross-workspace-visibility (PRD §10.3) are not stated to share that enum.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md Appendix A L449-450; DATA_MODEL.md L46 (defaultVisibility enum) vs L34 GCL projections; PRD §10.3 L677 cross-workspace-visibility: sanitized; no shared VisibilityLevel type defined
findings.proposed_fix: Define a single VisibilityLevel enum in §3 reused by Workspace.defaultVisibility, GclProjection.visibility, and note frontmatter, and add TS sketches for GclProjection and Approval (incl. Approval.expiry, which DATA_MODEL L29 implies but the summary omits).
